name: The Well's Memory
description: Elara has discovered that the village well transmits root memories during the new moon. She wants to drink deeply and learn the antidote to the poison — but the trance leaves her vulnerable all night.

DISCOVERED.journal_entry: Elara believes the well is more than a water source — it sits atop a root junction that transmits memories during the new moon. She wants to drink and enter a trance to learn the poison cure. She needs someone to guard her.
objectives: Speak with Elara about her plan at the well after nightfall

ACTIVE.journal_entry: I agreed to protect Elara during the new moon trance. She'll drink from the well at midnight. I need to keep her alive until dawn.
objectives: Meet Elara at the village well at midnight on the new moon
Guard Elara through the night until dawn

REFUSED.journal_entry: I refused to help Elara with the trance. She'll try it alone, or not at all.

PROTECTED_TRANCE.journal_entry: I guarded Elara through the night. The trance was harrowing — she convulsed, she screamed Silas's name, she wept. But she emerged at dawn with the knowledge she sought. She knows the antidote. And she knows something else: the priest at the chapel is her brother.

STOPPED_BY_PLAYER.journal_entry: I pulled Elara from the well before she could finish drinking. She's furious — a cold, quiet fury worse than shouting. She says I robbed her of the truth about her own father. She'll work from what fragments she has, but her knowledge is incomplete.

PLAYER_DRANK_INSTEAD.journal_entry: I drank from the well myself. Silas's memories flooded through me — his children, his sacrifice, his communion with the Heartwood. I carry a fragment of Silas now. I see root patterns in the soil and hear the forest's heartbeat. Elara will never forgive this.

ELARA_TRANCE_ALONE.journal_entry: Elara attempted the trance without protection. She survived, barely. Something found her in the dark. She won't say what.

FAILED_ELARA_DEAD.journal_entry: Elara is dead. Whatever the well might have told her is lost. The antidote, the family secret — all of it, gone.
